mnemonics: Very Clever People Decentralize Databases Securely (VCPDDS) for: Validators, Consensus, P2P Network, Distributed Ledger, Cryptography, Digital Signature)
keyPoints: A blockchain is a distributed, immutable ledger secured by cryptography
Consensus mechanisms (like Proof of Work or Proof of Stake) enable trust and agreement without a central authority
Transactions are bundled into blocks, which are cryptographically chained together to form a permanent history
commonMistakes: Believing all blockchains are public and permissionless (many are private/permissioned)
Thinking 'blockchain' and 'Bitcoin' are synonymous (Bitcoin is one application of blockchain technology)
Assuming data inside a block is encrypted by default (it is hashed for integrity, but often not encrypted for confidentiality)
